page: Microsoft Azure D8ds_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the x86_64 architecture. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs derived from 4 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor running at 4.2 GHz. The instance is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. For local storage, it includes a 472 GB local disk and is Premium Storage capable. The hypervisor layer is managed by Microsoft Hyper-V. The processor supports advanced instruction sets, including Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated vector and matrix operations.

In benchmark evaluations, the D8ds_v7 demonstrates strong single-core CPU performance, scoring 2277.87 in stress-ng single-core tests, placing it in the top 25%. Single-threaded compression and decompression tasks are highly efficient, with gzip compression reaching 35.05 GB/sec and bzip3 decompression reaching 15.08 GB/sec, both ranking in the top 10%. Multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth remain in the average tier, with stress-ng multi-core scoring 9302.94 and large-block read memory bandwidth reaching 89.09 GB/sec. Database operations and web serving throughput also perform at average levels, with Redis SET operations handling 15.0 million operations per second and static web serving throughput reaching 16.54 GB/sec. For small language model inference, the instance achieves a strong text generation speed of 270.06 tokens/sec on a 135M model, though larger 7B model generation speeds are average at 10.21 tokens/sec.

The qualitative cost efficiency of the D8ds_v7 is characterized by its balanced resource density, combining dedicated high-frequency Intel Xeon compute with a substantial 472 GB local storage allocation. This configuration avoids the premium cost of specialized memory-optimized or GPU-accelerated hardware while delivering strong single-threaded performance. The instance is well-suited for general-purpose workloads, relational databases, web serving, and lightweight machine learning inference tasks that benefit from AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets without requiring dedicated GPU hardware.
description: Microsoft Azure D8ds_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine configured with 8 dedicated vCPUs and 32.0 GB of RAM, running on an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor at 4.2 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance includes 472 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable. It features advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel AMX. In benchmark testing, the D8ds_v7 exhibits strong single-core performance, particularly in single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression, which rank in the top 10%. Multi-core performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations align with average tier expectations. This resource profile offers a balanced, cost-efficient solution for general-purpose applications, web serving, database hosting, and moderate vector or matrix computing workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D8ds_v7 is an 8-vCPU general-purpose virtual machine featuring an Intel Xeon processor, 32 GB RAM, 472 GB local storage, and strong single-core CPU and compression performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D8ds_v7 is a general-purpose VM with 8 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and 472 GB local storage, powered by a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon processor.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated high-frequency Intel Xeon compute, local storage, and strong single-threaded performance.
bullet_points: Powered by an 8-vCPU Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor running at 4.2 GHz
Equipped with 32.0 GB of RAM and a 472 GB local storage disk
Supports advanced hardware acceleration including Intel AMX and AVX-512
Delivers top-tier single-threaded compression and decompression benchmark results
Suited for general-purpose workloads, web serving, and database hosting
